untranslatable_reason: Signal requires calculation of lambda (λ) autocorrelation coefficient and half-life from price series, which are not available as pre-computed indicators. The rule depends on: (1) computing λ from returns autocorrelation, (2) deriving half-life from λ, (3) dynamically setting lookback period as a multiple of half-life. These require custom statistical calculations not expressible with the allowed indicator set. Threshold and exact multiplier are also AUTHOR_SILENT.
translation_notes: This is a meta-strategy rule about parameter optimization rather than a tradeable signal with fixed entry/exit conditions. To implement, backtest engine would need to: (1) calculate autocorrelation lag-1 of returns, (2) derive half-life = ln(2) / -ln(λ), (3) set lookback = k × half-life where k is typically 0.5-2.0, (4) apply mean-reversion strategies (pairs trading, Bollinger Band mean reversion, etc.) only when λ < 0 and half-life is reasonable (e.g., 5-100 bars). Context-dependent direction reflects that entry signals depend on deviation from mean rather than fixed long/short bias.
